behavior_summary: Handles the 'U8P_GHOSTHED::look' (examine) event for the Ghost Head item. When a player/actor inspects the object the script constructs the literal string "ghost head ", adjusts some item/process context (via 'set info' and 'process exclude' opcodes), and calls Item::bark to deliver that short description to the inspecting process. After the bark call the temporary string is freed and the routine suspends/returns.
notable_control_flow: Entry on the 'U8P_GHOSTHED::look' event: read a target process id parameter (from stack/BP+06h).
Perform 'set info' and 'process exclude' opcodes immediately before producing output — likely to set object state or visibility for the bark operation (inferred).
Create a transient string containing "ghost head ", push the target process id and the string pointer, then call Item::bark.
Free the temporary string, capture the bark return value, use it with an 'implies' operation, then suspend/return.
open_questions: Exact semantics and effect of the 'set info' and 'process exclude' opcodes in this context (they appear to alter object processing/visibility but the precise flags and downstream effects are not explicit).

Whether the constructed string ever incorporates dynamic data (for example the pid or other contextual info) beyond the literal "ghost head "; disassembly shows only the literal but push order hints at possible PID-related behavior.
Meaning and side-effects of the 'implies' instruction applied to the bark return value prior to suspend/return—how the return value influences engine behavior is inferred but not certain.

main_function.name: U8P_GHOSTHED::look
main_function.purpose: Respond to the item's 'U8P_GHOSTHED::look' event by producing a short descriptive bark/description for the Ghost Head and delivering it to the inspecting process/player.
entry_conditions: Event 0 (U8P_GHOSTHED::look) — invoked when an actor/player examines the Ghost Head object.

function_map.name: U8P_GHOSTHED::look
function_map.role: Main handler for the Ghost Head 'U8P_GHOSTHED::look' event; builds and emits the item's short description via Item::bark.

function_map.name: Item::bark
function_map.role: Engine/item routine used to display or emit a short text message from an item to a target process/player.

external_calls.purpose: Deliver the short description string (constructed as "ghost head ") to the target process/player (pid read from BP+06h).

When a player/actor inspects the object the script constructs the literal string "ghost head ", adjusts some item/process context (via 'set info' and 'process exclude' opcodes), and calls Item::bark to deliver that short description to the inspecting process. After the bark call the temporary string is freed and the routine suspends/returns.
